reviews.review_text:
You know, in a weird way this game really made me appreciate my faith in Christ even more lol. The predominant theme in this game is suffering, all to atone for your sins and appeal to the whims of some capricious, eldritch force called the Miracle. No matter what you do, though, your absolution is never guaranteed, and whether you do receive absolution or punishment, the end result is the same: more suffering, more pleading, never certain whether "enough" is enough, only watching as you deteriorate along with the rest of the world subject to the same cruel force. 

An enshrinement of transactional suffering and worship of a force that ultimately doesn't really seem to care about you.

Not so with Christ, who suffered for us so that we might not have to suffer, and who through His death and resurrection, guaranteed the atonement of our sins so that we might be reconciled to Him, a God who truly does care about us, hears us, and desires the best for us. 

Theological musings aside, though, this is a great game. I've never played too many Metroidvanias—my main one is Castlevania: Symphony of the Night, which sets a pretty high bar that Blasphemous is able to meet. The environmental imagery is grotesque in many ways but still evocative and beautiful due to its inspiration from Catholic Christian imagery and architecture. Each area successfully manages to create a feeling of foreboding, sorrow, reverence, and even adventure, due in no small part to the music. The music is definitely one of my favorite things about the game, and it really made me appreciate traditional Spanish music... something that's a bit personally relevant to me after growing up being exposed to Hispanic music styles like salsa, reggaeton, and norteña that I largely found obnoxious and grating. 

Obviously, music is largely a matter of taste, but it really was nice finally being exposed to a Hispanic musical style I actually really liked.

Being a Spanish speaker, I set the language (audio and text) to Spanish and was impressed by how good the voice acting was and how many new, if potentially archaic, words I learned lol. Even if you don't speak Spanish, you should definitely set it to Spanish still and just have the text in English, because the voice acting really does a lot for immersion and creating that feeling of being in an old, noble, and "foreign" place.

Finally, the gameplay. Movement is simplistic in design since it is a 2D game, but it doesn't feel clunky or outdated and instead feels smooth and responsive. Combat, mainly when it comes to the bosses, is challenging but fair, and it was fun figuring out their movesets to finally get a clean (clean enough at least lol) win in.

My main advice when it comes to exploration would be to make good use of the map marking system, because there will be PLENTY of areas and items that are inaccessible until much later in the game once you've unlocked  the necessary powerup to reach them. It's a fairly large map, too, so don't count on yourself to remember exactly what you missed and where.

Blasmeous is one of the best Metroidvania games out there.  Do not judge this game by its design.  It has some deep Mechanics and some interesting power ups and fun fighting style with good responsive platforming combing all this you will have a very good time playing it. Don’t afraid of the difficulty of this game. It’s not souls like once you learn the mechanics it will become more like an Action game.
My minor complaints are the story and world is very interesting but understanding it is hard. I have to watch youtube afterwards to make sense of it.  Some puzzles are hard to grasp to the point “I doubted this is even a puzzle”. Again I have to watch youtube to know what to do.
To sum it up this is a Fantastic game with awesome replayability. Believe me you have to try the new game+ modes it will completely change the way how you will play and approach this game. I played Hades game more than 200 hours but by final hours playing it I feel some kind of emptiness due to that I can’t finish it the way I wanted.  But finishing this game completely gave me a sense of satisfaction I don’t know way.  I also don’t know why I was hooked up to this game this much as well!!!

Indie developers once again putting the entire AAA industry to shame. Atmosphere, music, locations and bosses are on point. The pixel art is beautiful, the NPCs are really good and finding the collectibles is actually rewarding. The progression is good, you gradually feel like you're becoming stronger, and the combat is fun. I have no bad things to say except F*CK the time trials and the Shipyard, thankfully they're optional.

This game is a masterpiece and an instant classic

good game
great  music , design , exploration
not a great Metroid one no sense of progress no new ability except some new charms
7/10
It gets frustrating soo fast. Was enjoying it at the start until the point where i was playing it and asking "When does it end?" on every single fight.

A very solid take on a metroidvania style game with a little bit of Dark Souls thrown in the mix. Combat is simple yet challenging at times. Taking the time to master your kit is very much a requirement, you won't be blundering your way through this game unless you take the time to master the skills given to you. This is my second attempt at the game, the first time I played it I didn't get very far after the first boss. I kept making mistakes and I came to the conclusion that the game was too hard for me.

But here I am back at it about 12 hours into my current save file and I'm doing much better than I originally had. I will say that Blasphemous doesn't do a very good job in directing you where to go. There are a few times where I was just outright lost and had absolutely no idea where to go (much like my experiences with Dark Souls.) But with the few google searches I have done on my playthrough I was able to get myself once again going in the right direction without having to really spoil anything.

I can for sure say that this game is very much worth your time. It's also got a pretty reasonable price. That's on top of the fact that every single DLC aside from the OST and a cosmetic skin has been free. Buy this game and play it, I don't think you will be disappointed at all unless of course you don't like metroidvania games in the first place.

Both Blasphemous games are goated but I personally preferred this one. 
The atmosphere and exploration is memorable as well as the soundtrack and lore which great in both Blasphemous games. 
Would definitely recommend for any souls-like enjoyers who doesn't mind 2d games and pixelated graphics.

I enjoyed Blasphemous a lot. I really liked the dark fantasy setting, the lore, and the combat. The pixel art graphics are amazing and help create a dark and unique atmosphere. The world feels interesting to explore, and I enjoyed discovering more about the story and its characters.

One of my favorite things was the finishing moves on enemies. They look great, feel satisfying to pull off, and fit the game's brutal style perfectly. I also liked the puzzles, which add some variety and make exploration more rewarding.

The game is challenging, but I didn't find it too difficult overall. Most of the time it feels fair, and it only gets really tough if you're underleveled or missing important upgrades or key items.

Overall, I had a great time with Blasphemous. It combines great combat, interesting lore, beautiful pixel art, and fun exploration into a really memorable experience. I can't wait to play Blasphemous!
good game, great art and glorious cutscenes

OVERVIEW
--Cons--
Dialect
Endgame Completion

--Pros--
Achievements
Amazing Story
Amazing Graphics
Smooth PVE

THOUGHTS 
This game, like every metroidvania, has many things to do, which almost seems to be both good and bad. Collection and completion of the game requires you to play the campaign a multiple times in order to truly complete the game. The game also has a dialect of English(similar to Ole English) which you may not be familiar with, therefore u might need to search a few things to fully understand the story(A ingame glossary would have been a good addition). The story is amazing I've got no complains with how the pacing is crafted and the overall plot of the story. The connection of this game to theocracy creates this new image of Christianity and its different sides that were present in the middle ages. The graphics are insanely good, you can see the rough portrayal of emotions just like paintings in old time periods captured in pixel art. With how good the first iteration of this game, ill gladly grab Blasphemous 2!

This game is mediocre at best. My biggest problem with it is how confusing it is. Even though you can beat the game without specific abilities (relics), getting some of them without a guide is completely impossible. They have extremely cryptic methods of acquisition, which IMO is really annoying.

For example, the Nail Uprooted from Dirt requires you to do a specific quest where you need to find a random NPC called "Redento" five times throughout the game. I talked to him the first time and helped him move a bit, but I didn't speak to him after that. Therefore, the quest didn't continue, even after I explored the whole map and cleared the path he takes, because I didn't do that. Terrible game design.

Another problem I have with this game is that all the bosses (except Isidora, whom I really liked) are extremely easy. The final boss, the happy snake, everyone is easy, and at worst they take like 3 tries. But spikes are evil . For some ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ reason, someone thought it was a great idea to make spikes one-shot you. It's really annoying. The ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ spikes are clearly the final boss of this game.

I hate to say it, but this game is not good. I really wanted to like it, it reeks of style and developer passion. The game's music and atmosphere are top notch, however, the game just falls flat in the most important aspects: it feels awful. The controls are clunky and combat is weak. Now, most people will say, "it is a metroidvania game, of course it feels awful at the start." Unfortunately, no. I have played and loved many metroidvania games, including the originals. This game's controls are pretty awful feeling, no screw attack or double jump upgrade will fix the way the game moves and feels; it just lacks fluidity. I really wanted to like this game and am still happy to support them on this passion project and hope they make a second one that builds on this one, but this one is not great.

Sorry but one issue outweighs all the good things about this game. It has an interesting story, and overall a good game, but absolutely no controller support. I wanted to play through it a second time with an xbox series X controller, which steam says this game supports, but not only are half of the actions (like jumping) not there, the buttons in the settings for controls are numbered 1-9 instead of 'X', 'RB', 'LT' etc., and there is no button to make desired changes. I looked online and this appears to be an issue many people are having, and it wouldn't be a difficult fix, and we all know 2d souls-likes like hollow knight give the best player experience when played on controller. This alone turned this game from one deserving an automatic gleaming recommendation to a frustrated 4/10. Please fix this, then I will change my review.

Shortly after beating Esdras, a boss you fight at the end of what seems to be act 1 of this game, I asked myself: "am I actually having fun?" I then realized the answer was: "no, not really." 

I thought Esdras was particularly annoying to slog through, but I also realized that even though there were some good moments in the game for sure, overall I was finding it to be more tedious than actually compelling for me more often than not. 

A shame since it has phenomenal art direction and solid voice acting and the lore is quite compelling and yeah, even several components of the gameplay and level design are solid. But again, if as a whole I'm just not really having an especially good time with the game the majority of the time I'm playing it then, well...why bother? 

Maybe I'll come back to it again someday, but for now I think I'm over this game.

Disclaimer, I have not finished the game, but I believe I am close, I just want to put something out before I possibly forget to finish the game.

I'll get what I like out first
- the aesthetic and art are first and foremost the best parts of this game. It has beautiful pixel art with horrific bosses that are creative in design, and I wish there were more games that drew inspiration from catholicism.
-the combat is usually satisfying, each sword slash feels meaty and, while the block window is generous, it still feels good to pull it off. And the bosses have fun and interesting mechanics to have to deal with, which a lot of games don't quite get.
-the glory kills are fun to pull off a few times, don't remember what they replenish or give the player if anything.
-making the player not lose currency upon not being able to reclaim their spirit after death does mean you won't get set back from losing currency, but even then, this really only applies to low level gamers or first timers for souls-likes.

Now for what I don't like as much:
- the platforming feels repetitive. For as long as I've played, I don't believe any new platforming mechanics have been introduced yet. Sure there are different hazards that you have to avoid in each area, but there is nothing enhancing player mobility. Now, I'm not asking for the same exact mobility options that all metroidvanias seem to copy from one another, but I wish there was just one or two more mechanics added midgame to spice it up to keep the platforming from getting dry. Afterall, platforming is one of the main mechanics that makes this genre.
- I am not opposed to spiked pits in game, but there have been a few times where I just want to move and I end up dropping down onto a pit of spikes that were off screen. I think this also bleeds into my previous complaint as because of traps like this, you have to take platforming slow at times so as to avoid instant deaths  which I think makes the platforming feel like even more of a slog.
- it's common place to have a quick travel in all sorts of genres for good reason. While I don't mind backtracking as it is part of metroidvanias that make them non-linear and differentiates their gameplay, there is only so much backtracking a player is willing to do. So as more of the map is discovered, quick travel spots are discovered to help the player cut down on backtracking through areas they've been through a million times before. However, a key aspect of these quick travel points is location. You mainly want them to be in areas in which different branching paths are or where points of interest are. The issue in blasphemous is that the quick travel portals are out of the way of the player, usually making them a longer or more dangerous route than just traversing your way to where you want to go. I have probably only actually used the quick travel portals once or twice just to get back to the center of the map. I am dreading having to go back to previous areas because there isn't going to be any easy way to do it and that is why I'm afraid I won't finish this game even this close.
- there are also some sections where the hazards feel like bullcrap, like you are going to take some damage unless you are the best player ever. I'm not complaining about difficulty cause I've played multiple souls-likes at their intended difficulty, I'm just complaining of unfair/unreasonable difficulty.
-Souls-likes usually are known for story that is very complex but at the same time, not being in your face. For most it's fun to maybe piece together a little here or there to understand it and then deep dive into it later to fully appreciate the world. It feels like Blasphemous is trying to give you the deeper lore upfront to try and world build, but this usually results in long passages or monologues in old english that tends to confuse me or go over my head. While the story is interesting, I found it more enjoyable and engaging when I could actually understand it reading it off of the wiki instead of having it forced upon me confusingly. 

End summary:
It is not a bad metroidvania, I have enjoyed my time with it for the most part. I just think there are some glaring issues which hold it back from its true potential. I still want to finish it to see the game through, but it's going to be hard to bring myself to do it (part of the reason why I wrote this review was to do something other than play it while engaging with it so as to feel somewhat productive). I don't think I will be playing it again if I finish it, and I doubt I will ever play the sequel. If you are a hardcore metroidvania fan, you could try Blasphemous and maybe still like it a lot despite its flaws, but I'm probably a moderate metroidvania fan who likes the genre and has played a decent amount of the genre, but it is hard for me to continue one with bigger flaws.

It's pretty good. Stylistically, I like it more than other titles in the same genre. But how does the gameplay feel?

The Penitent One controls nicely. He's weighty in both his mobility and combat. Parries feel great, his attacks have a good flow, and he moves in a very expected manner. But you are completely useless in the air and against most projectiles. You also don't gain fervour fast enough to use the abilities a lot. Also I wish the Penitent One had a slightly larger sword.

I didn't find the game particularly difficult. Again, moments that require you to be in the air are you at your weakest. The platforming can be kinda tricky but that's mostly due to grabbing ledges and the wall climb.

I'm not a fan of the Dark Souls-inspired item descriptions for lore. This is doubled because many items have two descriptions. The world being inspired by a complex relationship with an Abrahamic-inspired religion in itself is very compelling and interesting. I just wish I could learn the Penitent One's purpose in the game world easier.

The base game's length seems under 10 hours which I stretched in trying to complete the map. But it felt very satisfying due to the sheer density of level design, exploration, and number of collectibles. You're rewarded for exploring throughout the map.
Es agrdable para mí, que no tengo una PC muy potente, poder jugar un juego tan bueno y entretenido como éste. 10/10
very good and very challenging. half these bosses suck ass but its still fun to fight them.

Religious theming go hard

only down side is that I didn't know quests were a thing and doing things in a specific order were a thing so don't just go killing things like actually follow the story.

The weight of Sin is quite heavy. Now this is one of those games is not the faint of heart of religious belief. As for the game i think the game is very decently made with its metroidvania side scrolling mixed with its dark fantasy and Spanish Catholicism themes. If they game doesn't crash on you. Playing as the The Penitent One is gives a scene of duty but also uneasy for the world is showing the constant suffering of its people which can feel little much like torture porn of a frustrated catholic male trying to gets its frustions out.I think the music give the game a atmosphere that's very unique. However there is a point in game for the noob filter that is uncessary difficult spike that was much harder than any of the base game bosses. There are 2 optional bosses to get the 3rd true ending the added as free dlc that are pretty good challenges. I will say the Anime rose lady side quests was tone killer for the game, super optional but for a medieval game it was a very jarring and didn't fit the theme. If you can get past the torture porn , than it's a decent advantage to play through once, I don't think I would come back to this game like darksouls or castlesvinia. Like my sins. I shall leave it in the past.
7.5/10
this game is awesome, challenging but not impossible and the visual style is just perfect

Most of the time its an exhilarating and badass experience with very unique and horrific bosses and enemies. The rest of the time it is pure ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ or clunky design and agonizing to have to go through. You get crazy awesome fire bosses fights like Quirce and then you get an electric piss stream of ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ such as serpies. It's a good game but rest in piece my controller it got hit by my chair.

I went into this game with an open mind thinking that it was going to be just like hollow knight, I was wrong. It may be too early for me to actually give an honest review for this game, but I am so done with it. The game is so slow at times giving you an abundance of quest items without an actual direction of how of where to use these items. I will probably give this game a second chance in the future and actually finish it but until then I do not recommend this game

this game is ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ gorgeous. i don't know what i thought when i first played but there is so much stuff that i adore.

music feels simple, but is very robust. each area with a different some and some of them are straight bangers.

graphically. it is all pixel art, but i don't know what black magic they used with this because it is so nice. everything is SO clean and easy to understand but having so much depth in it's detail that no matter what i did, i just stared at how good things are. this gets even more impressive when, some enemies are animated so smooth and dimensional that they had to have used some 3d references or something. ♥♥♥♥ is insane.

while i haven't done the true ending or whatever. nothing in the worlds makes any sense, but it all fits so perfectly and it feels right. all i can say is that you are one of MANY nameless sinners who's ultimate goal is probably to retrieve the first ancient relic to exist, but in order to do so, you must prove through your actions that you are pure of heart, as far as i assume.

gameplay is super crips and clean, very responsive. my only complaints about it could just be myself and my skill level at it. but sometimes trying to slide or perform sliding attacks just don't come out correctly. as well as the timed secret platform areas can get really stressful with how the wall grabbing mechanic works. but other than that, i really can't say anything negative about this. it is obvious that they put so much time and effort into this game and it shines.

Overall a good game that I would never recommend to anyone, there's a lot of passion in it, you can tell the developers really care about this game, I'm sure there's a lot of people who enjoy this game, but for me, the parts when I enjoyed my time were way too few compared to the times when I just wanted the game to be over, I don't remember any hard boss that I finally defeated after hours of frustration, but I remember being frustrated with Grievence Ascends to then defeat the Trio and just think "thats it ?"

The pixel graphics are beautiful, platforming can be frustrating at times but it was one of the things I enjoyed in the game, combat is just okay, not a single enjoyable boss, map is massive and unclear on where you're supposed to go.

Also couldn't care less about the story, it feels like a copout thats confusing on purpose to get loretubers to promote the game.
game pisses me off for how hard it is but the combat and what else to explore I guess makes it up for it to me

This game is truly stunning and the lore is in-depth and very interesting. I love the boss fights and the designs of each character. However, the gameplay was clunky at times and I came across quite a few bugs during my playthrough. Overall I very much liked my experience playing and I would recommend it, but its flaws should be acknowledged.

Dead instantly killed by a spike is the dumbest game design in this game. Why do you make a copy of Castlevania in the 21st century? The movement feels clunky, the map is small, and some enemies are cheap design while others are frustrating.
But I still want to give this a neutral review. The lore and story are pretty good if I understand English

Blasphemous was the first metroidvania and soulslike game that I ever played. And I AM THRILLED. 
Spanish gothic aesthetics with a mysterious plot that unravels up until the last second was not something I expected going into this. Curiosity of pixel graphics brought me in, but platforming, combat, puzzles and rewards are what me stay... NAY, made me want to play it again. The rerun itself was such a breath of fresh air, because of the game experience was so different the second time around. This game simply would not stop delivering new discoveries, and with different endings and juicy questlines. 
The artwork, the music, the aesthetic expression of the creators, coupled with satisfying gameplay makes this game a masterpiece. Really excited about what lies beyond the gates of Blasphemous 2 (already installed).

First playthrough was overall good and I enjoyed some of the boss fights.  I felt the difficulty level was uneven, sometimes it spiked to very hard and other times it was too easy.  I found the platforming elements tedious and frustrating at times, as you can die instantly when knocked into a spike pit or into a bottomless pit.  The common enemies were at times more challengin than the bosses because they could swarm you, or keep knocking you down with no way to move.  I beat some of the bosses first try, and some second try.  There were only a few I had to try many times, such as [spoiler]the red guard with a hammer, the female knight with sword, the singing woman in the ossuary and the snake in the pirate area, and the final boss.  However, most of these were a lot easier once I got all Mea Culpa upgrades and some of the better rosary beads and health upgrades.[/spoiler]
The item finding missions made no sense, and it was difficult to remember where everyone was, but the map and custom markers were useful for this.  I ended up with some unused key items as it was unclear that progressing past a certain point would terminate those quests.  I 'm really not a fan of games that do this, and force you to start over to complete a side quest.  It was a case of trial and error and most often I would give random items to people without realising I was meant to give the item to them because the explanations were so vague.  
Second playthorough I rage quit because the added difficulty and handicap made the game unenjoyable, which is a shame as I'll likely not get to see all the hidden bosses and alternative endings.  I have completed games which were technically more difficult than this, but I don't have the motivation to complete this several more times.  I also didn't like the ladder grab mechanic on some of the time trial levels; it just doesn't work reliably enough to put it into a timed challenge like that.  
If you just want to do a single playthrough, it's worth it when it's on offer, but new game plus isn't for me.

If you like 2D games and enjoy FromSoftware's souls games' medieval aesthetics and combat, this game is for you. It might be your new favorite Souls-like. The embedded narrative, hidden items and places, difficult bosses requiring multiple mechanical skills to kill are all there. The environment is amazing, dark and grim. The lore is beautiful as well. 

[b]I rate it 10/10 [/b]
had a blast playing this and getting all achievements. great gameplay, great story, great music, great art
Absolutely love the dark gritty 16-bit art-style, enemy designs and soulsborne like storytelling.

Blasphemous is a beautiful game with an immersive story that makes you question what is happening around you. The gameplay can be challenging but rewarding. The ending of this game hits hard no matter how you finish it. I can't recommend this game more to someone who has not yet tried this amazing game.

too many screens are just dull open spaces with enemies packed into them that aren't at all stimulating to kill over and over again. wished it was good because while the pixel art is stylized, its got a lot of detail and is nice to look at. wishing i refunded this while i was in the 2w timeframe

cool game, beautiful music , uniqe design , exploration, gore and dark story
great Metroid and i can't stop think this game for NG+ with true ending

i recomend for this game metroidvania lovers
for me 8,5/10

You know those platforming sections in Elden Ring where if you miss a jump by an inch you are sent hurtling into a bottomless pit and are instantly killed, forcing you to do the whole thing again?

Blasphemous is the answer to the question, "what if that was an entire game?"

Blasphemous is not just a game thou play, it is a wound thou carry, from the first step to the last gasp beneath the twisted sky an unholy vision of a penetant
this is beyond mortal praise and each death and requeam of sinners was a lesson curved into my bones

okay my out dated eng just finished, just a cuel ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ masterpiece
10/10

-Blessed be the pain.
Amazing story and gameplay, unique artstyle, fire music. 100% recommended if u like souls-like metroidvanias

Lovely game, enjoyed my time with it! I wasn't a fan of the art, (for some reason) when I started playing. Aftr a while I began to love it more and more.

Very fun game
Fun and challenging. Runs great even above default resolution.

Oh Blasphemous, where do I even begin.....

This game changed my life. I love Christian/Catholicism based games or Medieval type games and Metroidvania is got be my favourite genre. It's fun to discover little hidden secrets and fight unsettling bosses. Game design is epic and the game is not that challenging (trust me, I'm not that skilled) and is one of the more easier Souls-like games out there.

Similar games:
Dark Souls franchise
Nine Sols

Pros:
Amaze designs
Challenging but not arduous gameplay
Fun mechanics
Incredible story

Cons:
Couple of bugs in Miriam's challenges and some movement glitches if you have a certain rosary bead set combination equipped.

Highly recommend this game.

Honest opinion! The game is really fun, it's challenging and interesting. My only complaint is the fact that's its really easy to get lost and not know what to do next or where to go next. If that happens to you I simply recommend looking at guide or tips for beginner video to get you back on track before you rage quit. It really is a souls like game meets Metroidvania.
Love the aesthetics, gameplay is fun, story is definitely interesting so far despite not knowing what's really happening
This game is a lot of fun. The art direction is beautiful and the story is cool. I wouldn't recommend the New Game Plus mode, though, and it's lame that the devs locked content behind it.
Good game. Controls are somewhat clunky though but manageable.
This game feels like going to confession and then slashing your sins and then praying reaaaaaal good

What an amazing game. It is the first side view platformer I have ever played, and the first game I played on my steam deck. The music is so good, and the lore is very interesting. It's also constantly going on sale for 90%. It is so worth the money

I'm only 3 hours in, but this has been a lot of fun.  It's very souls like and I look forward to exploring more.

It's been running very well in Linux with no issues at all.
Great metrodivania, interesting mechanics and different style.

The game is amazing, works even on a terrible laptop or pc with the low requirements.
Most challenging game I played so far, 100%ed it in 45 hours or so... it's a lotta fun.
Amazing art style, love the characters and lore, will buy the 2nd game as well soon for sure.
If you are thinking about buying it, do it! You won't regret it!
Beautiful pixel art. Only thing that made me slightly uncomfortable was brutally murdering people dressed as members of my religion so some discretion is advised if you are Christian.
Great metroidvania souls-like. Very satisfying gameplay and amazing music.
I was captivated by the art style of this game, every game detail is perfect, especially, the boss fights. This game made me appreciate platformers even more.
I have defeated Sierpes twice and I will do it twice more. That snake deserves everything I have done and will do to it. Whoever designed that fight is evil and deserves the same treatment.

Beautiful game overall with great art, music, lore, and gameplay. 
It is a little hard to get the good ending and it is well worth it, but the ending boss wasn't as hard as some of the ones in the middle of the game. The spikes are unforgiving, but once you get past that, it's not too bad. The side quests are really good and really build the world around you without exposing too much of what's going on overall. I wish the game had more bosses and we could fight a certain someone who had something in their chest though.

Solid game, suitable challenge without giving you the middle finger for even trying. Haven't beaten it yet, but the game is rich with atmosphere. Although, all the characters speak with a kind of strange pseudo-king james bible kind of English, and everyone in this game is a damn poet, so I can't understand what the characters are talking about. The lore is pretty dense. The Miracle is ill-defined; I don't know what the hell anyone's talking about in this game, but I don't care. It's still fun. Satisfying gameplay. Recommend
What a very interesting world and pixelated graphics! Has a good amount of challenge and some good platformy sections. Highly recommend if you are into Metroidvanias.
Jogo deslumbrante. Metroidvania extremamente original na sua estética e com elevada qualidade no gameplay. A não perder.

True Torment its literally what it said :0
8.6/10 tho :3

Blasphemous is one of the most uniquely atmospheric metroidvanias ever made. The Spanish Catholic horror aesthetic is unlike anything else in the genre, dripping with grotesque religious imagery, tortured saints, and bleeding statues that all somehow look gorgeous in pixel art form. The combat is brutal and deliberate, the world of Cvstodia is haunting in the best way, and the lore goes absurdly deep if you're willing to dig for it. The Game Kitchen carved out their own corner of the genre with this one, and even years later there's nothing else quite like it.

[i]Blasphemous[/i] isn't a game for everyone. It features heavy gore, some nudity, and deeply disturbing creature designs that may not sit well with all players. However, if you don't mind, the game offers a truly captivating experience.

The soundtrack is beautiful, perfectly complementing it's dark & religious atmosphere. The art is incredible (detailed & unique). The gameplay feels satisfying and rewarding.

The boss difficulty is challenging but not overly stressful as long as you progress properly. Avoid jumping into areas too early, and make sure to hit a wall every map to discover a hidden area (this is the key). Upgrade mea culpa, bile flasks, and maximum health. With the right preparation, the game strikes a solid balance between challenge and fairness.

WOW. this game is one of the best games iv ever played i love how unique every boss is they all feel fresh i love how parkour mechanics are in boss fights and i love what they did with the beads you can basically make builds and cause of the large variety of beads and sword hearts you can make alot of unique and wacky builds truely a masterpiece and im so glad my friend got me into playing this the games also punishing and hard but if you make really op builds it can be very easy ALSO 3 FREE DLCS this game is amazing stop looking at reviews and buy the game already.

Solid metroidvania that I overall had a very fun experience with. The artstyle is what initially drew me in and held that beautiful quality and design throughout the entire playthrough. As with many games of this genre, there were moments where all I was doing was constant backtracking. Compounded with some areas and enemies being a slog to go through meant there were parts of the game that just weren't fun or engaging at all. I do plan to pick this back up in the future to try and achieve the other endings. Highly recommend if this game already seems up your alley, otherwise wait for a small sale to try it out.

5/5 I haven't even finished it and I already love it. I will finish it, I'm Just taking my time so the fun last longer. Combat is perfect and features accurate hit boxes. It will be challenging for most, but not ME. =) The "leveling system" is perfect, as it's rewarded for progression and exploration. It's very pretty too. They squeezed out A LOT of detail with the 2D pixel art. The theme is unique and makes you wonder why it isn't used more often. There is a reason why it's a well known game and it deserves its sequel. A MUST OWN AND OFTEN ON SALE FOR DIRT CHEAP. YOU HAVE NO EXCUSE. "*****" 5/14/26 I 100%ED it.

For a platforming game the platforming feels horrible and movement is far from fluid.  Visually you often feel like you should have landed on a platform but you have to be further in to the platform to actually land.  Often leading to fall into insta death spikes or into the void and respawning feeling frustrated. 

Half of the enemies are just annoying to deal with, the other half is just 1 hit trash. 

Why would you put the effect of the rosary beads at the bottom of the text so you have to scroll down every time you want to see what the items do? I don't really understand why you can only have 3 relics at a time, it just leads to a lot of unnecessary inventory shuffling. 

As for bugs, I haven't encountered many but there have been a few times where I wasn't able to use flasks.

As many others have said before, the art direction has a lot going for this game and is partly the only reason I pushed through until the end.
The atmosphere and art design are beautiful, but since I don't like souls-like games, I didn't like this one either.

Blasphemous is one of the most unforgettable games I’ve played. Its dark, gothic world feels oppressive, mysterious, and beautiful at the same time. Every location in the land of Blasphemous is packed with haunting religious imagery, towering cathedrals, decaying statues, twisted miracles, and disturbing creatures that make the world feel ancient and cursed. The pixel art is exceptional; every frame is filled with intricate details, from the texture of stone walls and rusted armor to the grotesque designs of bosses and enemies. The atmosphere is constantly enhanced by melancholic music, distant chants, and ambient sounds that create a sense of loneliness and reverence. Combat feels weighty and brutal, making every victory satisfying and every mistake costly. What truly sets the game apart is its storytelling—rather than explaining everything directly, it invites players to uncover its lore through exploration, item descriptions, and environmental details. The result is a world that feels alive with history, suffering, and mystery. Blasphemous is not just a Metroidvania; it is a work of dark art that combines stunning visuals, rich symbolism, challenging gameplay, and masterful world-building into an experience that lingers in the mind long after the credits roll.

A platformer/metroidvania much like HK but with Catholic themes. Strong points here are:

1. The combat, which is a bit slower and block/parry more useful/enjoyable
2. Exploration

Weak points would be:
- Story (unless maybe you are Catholic?)
- Graphics (pixel art is a style, but even so it really struggled to shine on my 4k monitor)

Overall very fun, hope to come back and finish ending C sometime

DIO MERDA QUELLA CAZZO DI AREA CO LE NAVI PORCA PUTTANA LA MADONNA LE BESTEMMIE CHE MI ESCONO. DIO BESTIA SE IL TITOLO DEL GIOCO E AZZECCATO. Apparte questo bellissimo gioco, consigliato se volete bestemmiare per degli stronzi rossi che vi tirano le ancore.

As an atmospheric connoisseur, this has been one of the solidest additions to my little library. The pixel art is a literal masterpiece! Every boss design and gothic environment makes it feel like an interactive art gallery. While the combat feel and brutal executions are extremely fun, it requires serious effort and mental dedication. However, the post boss hand tremors and the ultimate sense of accomplishment make it all worth it. The only catch is that the heavy religious allegories and guilt themes mildly triggered my childhood religious trauma. At one point, I found myself randomly repenting for my sins. Also, listening to gregorian chants in the background while playing hits incredibly different and turns the atmosphere up to eleven. (I recommend it to try) If you have the patience and an eye for high quality visuals don't miss out. 9/10.
